meaning: n.扰乱社会民心的人,散布谣言的人
examples: Baobab is no scaremonger, but in this case the charities who backed GCE, including Oxfam and Save the Children, would have done better to have concentrated minds rather than hearts.
The first minister accused the UUP of speaking "complete trash" and attempting to "scaremonger and to deceive people outside".
But is it coherent to scaremonger about food security in Europe and yet to call for less intensive (and thus less productive) agriculture?
